name: Huntsman

verdict.issue: Core mechanic is correct (conditional re-roll wound vs MONSTER/VEHICLE), but `subset: "all-failures"` restricts the re-roll to failed wound rolls only. The rule says "you can re-roll the Wound roll" with no restriction on the prior result — you may optionally re-roll any wound roll (success or failure) made in this context.

reasoning: OR compound on target-has-keyword MONSTER / VEHICLE is the exact restriction the draft omitted; the re-roll leaf is otherwise unchanged.

name: Brigand

verdict.issue: The `keyword-grant` uses `target: "unit"`, which grants Ignores Cover to the unit. The rule says "that attack has the [IGNORES COVER] ability" — it is a per-attack grant, not a unit-wide grant. Granting to the unit (even conditionally) means every attack meeting the condition elevates the whole unit's keyword set rather than applying Ignores Cover only to the specific triggering attack.

reasoning: attack-is-type:ranged gates the attack type; within-range-of-objective replaces the garbled placeholder — in attack-conditional context the condition evaluates against the target unit's position relative to objective markers.

name: Executioner
reasoning: Sequence adds the missing on-kill battle-shock step: units-destroyed{enemy, phase, 1} fires the reactive trigger; ability-grant:'battle-shock-test' on enemy-within-aura is the closest canonical encoding because leadership-modifier only enumerates 're-roll' as an operation, not 'force'. Scope aura-3 approximates the 3" measured from the destroyed unit — the grammar cannot distinguish bearer-origin from destroyed-unit-origin auras.

name: Stalker

verdict.issue: Core mechanic is correct (add 1 to wound when target is isolated). The condition uses `opponent-unit-within-range` with no exclusion parameter, but the rule specifies "no OTHER units from your opponent's army" — the target unit itself must be excluded from the within-6" check. If the DSL evaluates this check without implicitly excluding the target, the condition could never be satisfied (target is always 0" from itself).

reasoning: NOT(opponent-unit-within-range:6) encodes the isolation requirement — no other enemy unit may be within 6" of the target; the wound +1 only fires when that predicate is satisfied.

name: Protection Protocols

verdict.issue: The two key mechanics are captured (0CP cost + can reuse the stratagem this phase), but `cp-refund` models pay-then-refund rather than the rule's explicit cost-of-0CP. Additionally, `ability-grant: heroic-intervention-repeat-same-phase` does not encode the rule's "different unit" qualifier — the rule permits reuse only when the prior target was a *different* unit, not the same one again.

reasoning: cp-refund names the correct stratagem (heroic-intervention, replacing the placeholder); ability-grant:heroic-intervention-repeat-same-phase encodes the 'even if already targeted a different unit this phase' exception as a stratagem-repeat permission — two distinct mechanical grants, so sequence is required.

name: Karnivore

verdict.issue: Core mechanic is correct (re-roll Advance and Charge for this unit). However, `subset: "all-failures"` adds a restriction the rule does not state — the rule says "you can re-roll" with no qualification, meaning any result (high or low) may be re-rolled. Advance rolls have no failure state at all, making the subset semantically inapplicable. The re-rolls should be unconditional on the prior result.

reasoning: Sequence adds the missing advance re-roll step alongside the existing charge re-roll; roll:'advance' is structurally parallel to roll:'charge' in the re-roll leaf and captures the Movement-phase dice roll for advancing units.

name: Seething Hatred

effect.choice_label: re-roll one hit or one wound

reasoning: Wrapping in choice with no subset gives exactly one re-roll per option; omitting subset avoids the all-failures semantics the draft incorrectly applied. The or-compound condition fires in both shooting and fight, matching 'selected to shoot or fight'.

name: Searing Flames

verdict.issue: The rule grants the player an active selection from all enemy units hit by the Acheron flame cannon (potentially multiple). The DSL's `was-hit-by-attack` with `subject: target` binds the condition to the direct attack target rather than any unit hit during the model's shooting, slightly narrowing the eligible pool. Core mechanic — no Benefit of Cover in Shooting phase via Acheron flame cannon — is correct.

reasoning: was-hit-by-attack{subject:target} fires after the attacker's weapon hits an enemy, matching 'after this model has shot, select one enemy unit hit by those attacks.' Target 'defender' receives a no-benefit-of-cover grant rather than the draft's friendly-facing ability-grant — this reverses the polarity the verifier flagged. Medium confidence because the grammar has no dedicated cover-suppression leaf; ability-grant on the defender with a named grant_type is the most precise available encoding.

name: Unrestrained Terror (Aura)

verdict.issue: The condition chain's `operator: and` combines `unit-has-keyword: WAR DOG` and `engagement-state` as if they apply to the same referent, but the rule involves two distinct entities: a friendly WAR DOG within 6" (handled by scope.range) and an enemy within Engagement Range of that WAR DOG. Collapsing these into a single condition sequence creates ambiguity — it could be read as checking the enemy for WAR DOG keyword. The core effect (forced Battle-shock at start of Fight phase) is correct.

reasoning: unit-has-keyword(WAR DOG) adds the qualifier the draft omitted — only WAR DOG units in the aura-6 are the active proximate source. engagement-state on those WAR DOG units encodes 'Engagement Range of one or more WAR DOG units affected.' target enemy-within-aura approximates the transitively-close enemies; a dedicated within-engagement-range-of-keyword target does not exist in the grammar. operation:force is an extension of the listed 're-roll' operation to cover forced test imposition.

name: Methodical Destruction
reasoning: Sequence captures all three mechanics: initial victim selection at battle-round 1 via ability-grant{victim-designation}, wound re-roll while the victim is active, and reselection triggered by units-destroyed{enemy}. The grammar has no 'target is designated victim' condition, so the re-roll step cannot be precisely filtered to attacks against the victim — this is the residual approximation. player-turn-is{turn:1} combined with timing-is{start-of-battle-round} approximates 'first battle round only' for initial selection.

name: Close-range Killers (Aura)

verdict.issue: All elements correct: aura-9 range, WAR DOG keyword condition, closest-eligible-target attack condition, AP subtract 1 (correct direction for 10e AP notation), friendly-within-aura target, permanent duration for an always-on aura.

reasoning: unit-has-keyword(WAR DOG) gates the aura to WAR DOG models only. attack-is-type{closest-eligible-target} is the closest available encoding for 'targets the closest eligible target' — no dedicated condition type exists in the grammar for proximity-ordered targeting. operation:subtract with value:1 is cleaner notation than add:-1 for improving AP. Medium confidence due to the attack-is-type approximation.

name: Obsessive Ruthlessness

verdict.issue: All elements correct: ranged attack condition, MONSTER-or-VEHICLE OR condition, Devastating Wounds keyword grant scoped to ranged weapon type, self target.

reasoning: The draft lacked any target restriction; the fix adds a nested or-compound on target-has-keyword(MONSTER) and target-has-keyword(VEHICLE) inside the outer and-compound with attack-is-type(ranged). This precisely gates Devastating Wounds to ranged attacks against MONSTER or VEHICLE targets as the rule states. weapon_type:ranged in the modifier is redundant with the condition but makes the narrowing explicit for consumers.

name: Bastion of Firepower

verdict.issue: Trigger (remained-stationary), keyword-grant (Lethal Hits on ranged weapons of bearer), and scope (self/turn) all match the rule exactly.

reasoning: Wraps the draft's keyword-grant in a remained-stationary condition; weapon_type:ranged scopes to ranged weapons only; duration:turn captures 'until the end of the turn'.

name: Macro-extinction Protocols

verdict.issue: Outer condition (MONSTER ∨ VEHICLE) → +1 hit, nested (TITANIC ∨ TOWERING) → +1 wound correctly represents 'as well'; nesting ensures wound bonus only fires when the outer condition also fires.

reasoning: Hit +1 applies to all MONSTER/VEHICLE targets; the wound +1 is an inner conditional on TITANIC/TOWERING, so it stacks only when both conditions are true — the nested structure replaces the draft's flat sequence.

name: Consumed with Hunger (Aura)

verdict.issue: subset: 'all-failures' is a phantom condition. The rule says 'you can re-roll the Hit roll' — an unconditional re-roll of that die regardless of whether it succeeded or failed. Restricting to failed rolls adds a restriction the rule does not state.

reasoning: The unit-has-keyword:WAR DOG condition narrows which friendly models benefit from the aura; the target-has-keyword TITANIC/TOWERING condition restricts which attacks trigger the re-roll — both were absent from the draft.

name: Sunderer of Fortresses

verdict.issue: The rule's parenthetical ('if that attack targets a FORTIFICATION') refers to the attack already identified as targeting a VEHICLE, so nesting is correct. Accumulative +1+1 = +2 total is mathematically identical to 'by 2 instead'.

reasoning: The grammar has no else-branch; FORTIFICATION is a subset of VEHICLE, so a base +1/+1 for all VEHICLEs plus an additive inner conditional +1/+1 for FORTIFICATION yields the correct totals (+1 vs +2) without inventing new node types.

name: Storm of Bolts

verdict.issue: Core mechanic and conditions (was-hit, not-MONSTER, not-VEHICLE, -1 to hit) are correct. Missing the rule's 'while this model is on the battlefield' persistence condition, which can terminate the suppression if the bearer is destroyed before the effect expires.

reasoning: was-hit-by-attack {subject:target} models 'that enemy unit was hit by bearer's attacks'; the reactive frame then fires when that unit (now attacker) makes its own attacks, applying -1 to hit; NOT MONSTER/NOT VEHICLE reflects the selection restriction; duration:battle-round captures 'until start of your next turn' spanning through the opponent's turn.

name: Shock Charge

verdict.issue: The rule says Tank Shock costs 0CP; the DSL models it as spend-1-then-refund-1. Net CP cost is the same, but the refund model would incorrectly block usage when a player has 0CP — a case the rule permits. The phase-limit bypass is correct.

reasoning: Splitting into two leaves — cp-refund (amount:1, making the net cost 0) and ability-grant naming the phase-limit bypass — is cleaner and more parseable than a single monolithic grant_type string that bundles both mechanics opaquely.

name: Bloodlust

verdict.issue: Core mechanic correct: charged-this-turn condition gates a Devastating Wounds keyword grant to melee weapons for the turn. Scope self/turn matches 'until the end of the turn'.

reasoning: The Charge move is a standard game-state trigger expressible as charged-this-turn; wrapping the keyword-grant in a conditional and narrowing to melee weapon_type fully captures the rule.

name: Repair Auto-simulacra

verdict.issue: Effect type 'resurrection' is wrong. Resurrection means returning a destroyed model from casualties; the rule says 'regains up to D3 lost wounds', which is wound healing on a living model. Should be a heal-wounds (or restore-wounds) effect type.

reasoning: The grammar has no dedicated heal-wounds leaf; resurrection is the closest available type and is distinguished from model-resurrection by modifier type:wounds — the primary fix here is adding the end-of-Command-phase timing condition that the draft omitted entirely.

name: Warp Storms (Psychic)

verdict.issue: Core mechanic correct: end-of-Movement-phase trigger, D6 per enemy within aura-9 on 3+ deals D3 mortal wounds. Scope aura-9 accounts for the '9" of one or more models with this ability' range.

reasoning: The end-of-Movement-phase timing gates a dice-gated D6 ≥ 3+ check that, on success, inflicts D3 mortal wounds to each enemy within the 9" aura — the aura target encodes the per-enemy-unit iteration implied by the rule.

name: Grav-pinned

verdict.issue: Core mechanic correct: shooting-phase trigger with was-hit-by-attack (graviton crusher, ≥1 hit) and INFANTRY keyword gate; sequence applies Move −2 and charge-roll −2 to the defender. Duration battle-round correctly maps to 'until end of your opponent's next turn' when applied during your turn (= rest of current battle round).

reasoning: The reactive trigger fires in the Shooting phase when a graviton-crusher attack hits an INFANTRY target; the two-step sequence captures both the -2 Move characteristic and the -2 Charge roll modifier that constitute the grav-pinned state, lasting until end of the opponent's next turn (battle-round duration).

name: Code Chivalric
reasoning: The pregame Oath-selection procedure and the conditional 3CP bonus for random selection exceed what the grammar can represent, but the three Quality stat-effect options and the three Deed completion triggers with cp-gain plus Honoured keyword-grant are encoded as closely as the available condition types permit; the once-per-game Deed completion guard also has no grammar expression.

name: Bondsman

verdict.issue: The rule requires the target to be a friendly ARMIGER model — the ARMIGER keyword restriction is entirely absent from the authored DSL, which instead targets any friendly-within-aura. This is a stated targeting condition the rule explicitly imposes that is missing from the DSL.

reasoning: The Command-phase activation that delivers a Bondsman ability to a friendly unit within 12" is captured via phase-is condition plus ability-grant on friendly-within-aura; the ARMIGER keyword eligibility restriction and the 'not already bonded' exclusion cannot be expressed in the target or condition grammar.

name: Freeblades

verdict.issue: Three problems: (1) The condition is `unit-has-keyword: IMPERIUM`, which checks whether the ability-bearing unit has the IMPERIUM keyword. The rule requires that *every model in the army* has IMPERIUM — an army-scope predicate, not a unit-scope one. This is a core-mechanic error because the gate misfires entirely. (2) The rule's explicit restriction that the included models cannot be the WARLORD is absent. (3) The rule's explicit restriction that the included models cannot receive Enhancements is absent. Both are stated conditions, not implied ones, and their omission leaves the modeled effect incomplete.

reasoning: This rule is entirely army-construction / roster-selection: it controls which models may be included in a list (bypassing the faction-keyword requirement), caps their count, prohibits WARLORD designation, and blocks Enhancements — all pre-game constraints with no in-battle effect. Four distinct gaps prevent full encoding: (1) the grammar has no condition for 'every model in the army has keyword X' (unit-has-keyword is per-unit, not an all-friendly sweep); (2) count caps (one TITANIC, up to three ARMIGER) are army-construction limits the grammar cannot model; (3) WARLORD prohibition is an explicit unencodable roster-selection rule; (4) Enhancement restriction is list-building, not a battle effect. The best partial tree encodes the IMPERIUM precondition (approximated) and the two-way choice between inclusion grants, but consumers must not treat these ability-grant leaves as in-battle effects.
